title: Policies
text: ### Acceptable Use Policy Read the NRP Acceptable Use Policy (AUP). :::danger[You need to accept the AUP before using the cluster] ::: ### Resource allocation 1. Set the resource (particularly CPU, memory and ephemeral-storage) `limits` properly. Your resource `limits` must be within 20% of the resource `requests`. 3. While running a large number (> ~100) of pods or jobs, you must choose your resource `limit` = `request`. 4. If a pod goes over its memory limit, it will be killed (OOM - Out of Memory). 5. If a pod exceeds the CPU limit, it puts pressure on the node. It can affect the whole node and other use's work. :::tip It is crucial to set `limits` correctly, but it is equally important not to set `requests` too high. - Aim to match your `requests` closely to the average resources you expect to use, while setting `limits` slightly above your highest anticipated peak. - Use monitoring to fine-tune these values effectively. ::: 6. Do not waste resources. Namespaces with consistently underutilized requests risk being banned. ### Resource usage violations 1. A user can not submit **more than 4 pods**, violating the following conditions: - **GPU** utilization is more than 40% of the requested GPUs. - **CPU** usage is within 20-200% of the requested CPUs. - **Memory (RAM)** usage is within 20-150% of the requested memory. 2. These restrictions **do not apply** to pods requesting **1 CPU core and 2GB memory**. 3. Keep checking the **Violations** page on the Nautilus portal to see if your pods are violating the usage policies. ### Interactive use (6 hours max runtime)
